taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Data Analysis, Data Scientist
core_skills: SQL, Excel, Python, Spark, Data Visualization
skill_match_scores: SQL:1.0, Excel:1.0, Python:1.0, Spark:1.0, Data Visualization:1.0
must_have_quals: Minimum 5+ years of relevant work experience in an analytical, technical or commercial field, Bachelor's Degree in a highly numerate major (Math, Stats, Econ, etc.) or relevant experience in data and/or business analysis, 1+ years managing and coaching analysts, data scientists and/or data engineers, Ability to organize and prioritize workload for a team of direct reports, Proficiency in SQL and Excel required, Demonstrated experience using data visualization software to convey analysis in an easy to digest format, Strong verbal and written communication skills - demonstrable track record of influencing stakeholders and colleagues
preferred_quals: Basic ability to use other programming languages for data analysis such as Python, Spark preferred, Experience working with Tableau for data visualization preferred, Experience collaboratively working with colleagues in other countries and time zones preferred, Experience working within a heavily regulated industry is a plus
core_responsibilities: Manage a team of analysts to produce specific insights around behavior, year over year trends, and drivers of risk, Own tools and models that govern measurement of business performance, ensuring they are complete, accurate and regularly reviewed and refreshed, Audit, review, and help guide the development of our reporting suite to meet stakeholder needs in an effective and efficient manner, Review team's output ensuring insights are summarized and tools are checked before communication to stakeholders, Own the day to day management of requests and tasks of your direct reports, Ensure projects and resources are aligned with current departmental priorities, Provide management, coaching and professional development to analysts in your team, Identify and grow top talent within the analytics team
job_summary: FanDuel is the premier mobile gaming company in the United States and Canada, seeking a Manager of Analytics to lead a team focused on Fraud & Payments within the Security, Risk & Compliance Analytics team. The role involves managing analysts to provide insights, improve business performance, and reduce risk through actionable data analysis.
